_note: Measured per-field byte distribution from the 2026-07-19 incident run (465ff1957fab1a60d8ffde6e95e19925a1d963a0a2781bdf9d30ed1ece6a172e). The real task file is an internal product audit and is deliberately not committed here — generate.py rebuilds a synthetic, shape-matched payload from these numbers so the incident's two root causes stay reproducible without the original file. See docs/incident note '2026-07-19-argv-e2big-and-coverage-freeze.md' (Reproducing section) for the full write-up.

reconciler_payload._measured_component_sum_note: Sum of the incident note's 'Root cause B' component table (task + subtask_views + categories, each measured standalone). The fully-wrapped RECONCILER INPUT: user_prompt generate.py builds is larger still (adds JSON structural overhead: keys, the RECONCILER INPUT: header, the trailer sentence) — generate.py does not force the wrapped string to this exact figure, since doing so would require inflating task_bytes past its measured value. What matters for the regression is that the wrapped prompt exceeds MAX_ARG_STRLEN (131,071) on these measured component sizes alone, which it does with room to spare.

uncoverable_backtick_must_headings: Run `pnpm run lint:fix` - MUST pass with no errors
Run `pnpm test <touched>` - MUST pass with no errors
other_headings: Confirm TypeScript strict mode compliance (explicit return types)
Confirm error boundaries wrap new async components
Check that new API routes validate input with zod
Verify feature flags default to off
Check that new env vars are documented in .env.example
Confirm telemetry events use the shared event schema
Verify accessibility: interactive elements have aria labels
Check that new components have Storybook stories
